name: SafePay CMSTPLUA COM Object UAC Bypass
description: Detects PowerShell or pwsh.exe invoking the CMSTPLUA auto-elevated COM interface (CLSID 3E5FC7F9-9A51-4367-9063-A120244FBEC7) via GetTypeFromCLSID or System.Activator::CreateInstance to silently bypass UAC. This technique is used by SafePay ransomware and other threat actors to gain elevated execution without displaying a UAC prompt. The CLSID and API calls are inherent to the technique and will appear regardless of the tooling used.
query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and process.args : ("*3E5FC7F9-9A51-4367-9063-A120244FBEC7*", "*CMSTPLUA*", "*GetTypeFromCLSID*", "*CreateInstance*")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: SafePay Registry Run Key Persistence IoC
description: Detects creation of SafePay-specific registry autostart entries: value name 'SafePayService' or value data '6F22-C16F-0C71-688A' written under the HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run key. These strings have no known legitimate use and constitute a high-confidence SafePay IoC. A real-world attacker using the same technique will produce identical registry artifacts.
query: registry where event.type in ("creation", "change") and registry.path : "*\\CurrentVersion\\Run*" and (registry.value : "SafePayService" or registry.data.strings : "*6F22-C16F-0C71-688A*")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Suspicious Registry Run Key Write from User-Writable Process Path
description: Detects any process residing in a user-writable directory (Temp, AppData, Public, Downloads) writing to Windows autostart Run or RunOnce registry keys. Legitimate software installers that create autorun entries typically execute from Program Files or Windows directories. This generic pattern covers T1547.001 malware behavior independently of threat-actor-specific IoCs.
query: registry where event.type in ("creation", "change") and registry.path : ("*\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\*", "*\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce\\*") and process.executable : ("*\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*") and not process.executable : ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\*", "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Windows Defender Disabled via Set-MpPreference
description: Detects PowerShell or pwsh.exe invoking Set-MpPreference with flags that disable Defender real-time monitoring, behavior monitoring, IOAV protection, or block-at-first-seen. This is the most common programmatic method used by ransomware families — including SafePay — to impair endpoint defenses before deploying their payload. The cmdlet name and disable flags are intrinsic to the technique regardless of what drives them.
query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and process.args : "*Set-MpPreference*" and process.args : ("*DisableRealtimeMonitoring*", "*DisableBehaviorMonitoring*", "*DisableIOAVProtection*", "*DisableBlockAtFirstSeen*", "*DisableIntrusionPreventionSystem*", "*DisableScriptScanning*")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: SafePay Attack Chain: UAC Bypass Followed by Registry Persistence
description: Sequence detection correlating a CMSTPLUA-based UAC bypass attempt with a subsequent Run key registry write on the same host within a 5-minute window. The chained sequence dramatically reduces false positives and maps directly to the SafePay kill chain: privilege escalation via COM abuse followed immediately by establishing persistence. Any threat actor following this same playbook will trigger this rule.
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=5m
  [process where event.type == "start" and process.name : ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and process.args : ("*3E5FC7F9-9A51-4367-9063-A120244FBEC7*", "*CMSTPLUA*", "*GetTypeFromCLSID*")]
  [registry where event.type in ("creation", "change") and registry.path : ("*\\CurrentVersion\\Run*", "*\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce*")]
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator
